observation.summary: The daemon migration root remained the last registered manual document. It preserved compatibility markers and links, while all focused child migration sections were already generated from migrationdocs SSOT.

hypothesis.summary: A one-page migration-docs manifest can generate the daemon migration root, preserve compatibility markers consumed by workflows, and point readers to generated child migration sections.

execution.summary: Generate the daemon migration root from a fragment with source checks for child manifests, workflow-owned compatibility markers, Figma boundary, provider validation, and Store review ownership.

evaluation.summary: The verifier rejects doc drift, missing child manifest anchors, missing compatibility marker owners, and missing public CI evidence upload.

retrospective.summary: The daemon migration root now becomes executable evidence, leaving no registered manual docs in executable knowledge coverage.

evidence.proves: daemon migration root is generated and source anchors remain present
public CI uploads daemon migration root evidence
